methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Atom is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Tyler Hayes that has raised $11M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, and Ultima Genomics, though the private set is dispersed across multiple stages and valuations.

Given data constraints, only one quantitative method is defensible. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $11M raised as a proxy for current value, implying a post-seed valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 occupy the same stage or adjacent stages with comparable valuations, making a narrow median unreliable. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Atom's revenue is not disclosed, preventing use of the 6.1x median P/S multiple observed across the 3 public biotech comps.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, centered at $28M. This range reflects typical step-up expectations for Seed companies progressing toward Series A. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer financials at Atom's stage and lack of revenue visibility, both of which would strengthen conviction in the implied range. The estimate relies entirely on stage-based convention rather than market comparables or fundamental metrics.
